title: arenaria trinervia subsp. pentandra
intro: Arenaria trinervia subsp. pentandra, also known as a coastal or sea sandwort, is a subspecies of the Arenaria trinervia plant, which belongs to the carnation family and is native to the Mediterranean region. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties, which are attributed to its high concentration of flavonoids and other bioactive compounds. The health benefits of Arenaria trinervia subsp. pentandra include its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing, as well as its ability to exhibit antioxidant and immunomodulatory effects. Arenaria trinervia subsp. pentandra can be used in herbal preparations such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to treat various health conditions, including skin problems and respiratory issues.
